scope.standard_6_unconditional_scan: commands/*.md + agents/*.md (per spec exemption — Std 6 always full-repo scan even in --changed-files mode)

prior_critical_resolved.prior_finding: Verbatim Chinese phrase 禁止加载任何非本cycle的内容 in commands/ scope (Std 6 violation; only docs/dev/ticket-*.md is exempt)
fix_verification: grep literal Chinese phrase in commit.md: 0 matches
grep full Unicode CJK range U+4E00-U+9FFF in commit.md: 0 matches

Attribution clause at commit.md:135 uses English paraphrase + path-only cross-ref: 'This operationalizes the user binding directive that prohibits loading any non-current-cycle content (verbatim Chinese phrasing preserved at docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md, Standard 6 exemption scope).'
docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md exists (83 KB) and retains verbatim Chinese in Std 6 exempt scope

advisory_pre_existing_unchanged.rationale: Hook execution context where venv activation is structurally impossible (hooks run synchronously in Claude Code's stdin pipeline before any shell init). Pre-existing in prior cycles; was advisory in prior report; not introduced or modified by this cycle in a way that would change classification.
Six files in repo carry merge-conflict markers but NONE intersect this cycle's --changed-files list. Out-of-scope per close.md AC-2.6(b) line-level newness rule (no overlap with cycle diff).

Pre-existing verbatim user-binding/spec quotes with attribution to source spec/ticket. Files are in --changed-files, but these lines were not added by this cycle's fix-iter1 diff. Per close.md AC-2.6(b): a finding is NEW only when its line falls within the cycle diff's changed-line range AND is absent from the pre-diff baseline. These satisfy neither. Each carries a structural attribution (USER_VERBATIM tags or spec citation) that aligns with Std 6 exemption pattern.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex Bash invocation blocked by pretool-tool-policy.py (PreToolUse hook). Deny reason from hook: 'BLOCKED by tool-policy.v1: {"role":"style-inspector","tool":"Write","target":"$CODEX_OUT","deny_reason":"write target $CODEX_OUT not in allowed_write_path_prefixes"}'. The codex skill uses `tee` to write its output file to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/, which the style-inspector role policy does not whitelist. Same pattern as prior round in this cycle. Self-review substituted per inspector contract fallback clause.

findings.issue: Std 6 fix wording — could readers misinterpret the bracketed ticket reference at commit.md:135 as 'loading' non-English content?
findings.rationale: Self-review judgment: backtick-wrapped file path is a content-neutral markdown identifier, not content text. Std 6 exemption clause explicitly sanctions 'English with task-id attribution citing the ticket where the verbatim text is preserved' — this clause is satisfied. Path reference is not Std 6 'text content'.

findings.issue: Are there NEW Std 6 introductions elsewhere in the 25-file changed-files list?
findings.rationale: Self-review grep on commands/*.md + agents/*.md confirms remaining Chinese in close.md and dev.md is pre-existing verbatim user-binding/spec quotes with structural attribution (USER_VERBATIM tags or spec citation). These lines are not within this cycle's fix-iter1 diff hunk.

findings.issue: Should pre-existing Std 3 (python3 in hooks) advisory escalate to critical given this cycle modifies hooks?
findings.rationale: Self-review: the python3 invocations are structurally required for hook execution (hooks cannot activate venv before they run; they receive stdin from Claude Code's hook pipeline). Severity unchanged from prior cycle's advisory classification.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted (codex blocked by tool-policy); 3 adversarial questions answered; no escalation warranted

verdict.recommendation: CLOSE-clean from style-inspector perspective. Prior CRITICAL Std 6 violation at commands/commit.md:130 fully resolved by replacing verbatim Chinese with English paraphrase + path-only cross-reference to docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md (Std 6 exemption scope).
